title: Expand to additional low-resource languages
context: The M2 label target (≥3 languages) is a floor, not a ceiling. Low-resource languages — those with few digital tools, limited machine-translation quality, and high populations of low-literacy or displaced speakers — are the highest-need targets for vetted label sets. Expanding to additional low-resource languages requires finding fluent reviewer volunteers or partners for each language, since raw machine-translation cannot be shipped and low-resource languages have higher MT error rates. This task is backlogged until fluent reviewers are identified per locale.
objective: Expand the vetted label set to additional low-resource languages (beyond M2's ≥3 language baseline), each reviewed by a fluent speaker with provenance recorded, contingent on identified fluent reviewers.
acceptanceCriteria: Labels delivered for at least one additional low-resource language beyond the M2 baseline; language choice is documented and linked to an identified fluent reviewer and a clear beneficiary population need.
Each label reviewed by a fluent speaker of that language; reviewer name/role/locale recorded in metadata; no raw machine-translation shipped.
Where the language has limited MT tooling, the review process accommodates direct human-authored labels (not MT-then-review); the method used is documented in reviewer provenance.
Labels are published in labels/<lang>/ with reviewer provenance, versioned, and licensed CC-BY-4.0.
Symbols remain fully usable without the label; the label expansion does not alter any SVG source or comprehension testing records.
resources: open-pictograms-translation-002 (M2 labels in ≥3 languages — build on existing)
open-pictograms-data-001 (per-symbol metadata schema — labels field with reviewer provenance)
output: labels/<lang>/ (additional low-resource language label files with reviewer provenance) integrated into per-symbol metadata records; versioned and licensed CC-BY-4.0. PR opened against main per language batch.
